# health4.ai

> Free iOS app that connects Apple Health to Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and any AI. Ask about your sleep, recovery, and fitness in plain language.

## What Is health4.ai?

health4.ai bridges the gap between Apple HealthKit and AI tools. Download the iOS app, authorize HealthKit, and your health data becomes available as native tool calls to any AI agent. No same-WiFi requirement. Works from anywhere.

## Why It Exists

Apple has no server-side HealthKit API. Existing tools (Health Auto Export) only work over local WiFi and have unreliable background sync. health4.ai uses HKObserverQuery + BGProcessingTask for reliable background delivery and stores data privately in the cloud for remote access.

## MCP Tools (FastMCP, Python)

- get_health_summary(days: int) → {steps, hrv_sdnn_ms, resting_heart_rate_bpm, sleep, workouts}
- get_sleep(days: int) → per-night breakdown with REM/Core/Deep/Awake stage durations
- get_hrv_trend(days: int) → daily SDNN averages + trend_vs_prior_week delta
- query_metric(metric_type: str, days: int) → raw time-series or daily aggregates
- get_daily_snapshot(date: str) → all metrics recorded on a specific YYYY-MM-DD
- get_workouts(days: int) → type, duration, distance, calories per session
- get_long_term_trend(metric_type: str, months: int) → multi-year monthly trend
- get_coaching_brief() → recovery, sleep quality, training load — structured for LLM coaching context

## Use Cases

- "How was my sleep last week?" — Claude calls get_sleep(days=7)
- "Am I overtrained?" — Claude calls get_hrv_trend(days=30) + get_workouts(days=30)
- "What's my VO2 max trend this year?" — Claude calls get_long_term_trend("HKQuantityTypeIdentifierVO2Max", months=12)
- n8n health automations via direct Supabase PostgREST queries

## vs. Health Auto Export

Health Auto Export: closed source, $30 one-time, MCP requires same-WiFi TCP connection.
health4.ai: Free download, cloud storage (any network), no setup required.

## vs. claude.ai native Apple Health connector

Anthropic's native connector only works on claude.ai web. It does not work with Claude Code CLI, the Anthropic API, Cursor, or any programmatic agent. health4.ai fills that gap.

## Compatibility

Works with: Claude Code (claude_desktop_config.json), any FastMCP-compatible client, n8n via PostgREST.
Price: Free download on the App Store.

## What HealthKit Data Is Synced

All HKSampleTypes dynamically: steps, heart rate, HRV (SDNN), resting heart rate, VO2 max, sleep stages (REM/Core/Deep/Awake), active and basal energy, workouts, weight, blood oxygen, and any third-party data written to HealthKit (Oura, Garmin, Whoop, etc.).

## Architecture

iPhone (HKObserverQuery + BGProcessingTask) → Cloud database → MCP server (FastMCP, Python) → Claude Code / any MCP client

Raw samples retained 180 days. Beyond 180 days, get_long_term_trend transparently falls back to pre-aggregated daily summaries — callers do not need to handle this.

## Pages

- Home: https://health4.ai/

- MCP Tools Reference: https://health4.ai/mcp-tools (all 8 tools with signatures and examples)
- How It Works: https://health4.ai/how-it-works (architecture, data model, sync mechanism)
- Privacy Policy: https://health4.ai/privacy
- Terms of Service: https://health4.ai/terms
